title: oxalis psoraleoides
intro: Oxalis psoraleoides, also known as the wood sorrel or creeping wood sorrel, is a perennial herb native to South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Oxalis psoraleoides can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
